english: frame-work, sbrá-pa (sbra pa) inmate, of such a tent. 2. v. sub ytsań- (gtsang).

pf. sbrags (sbrags), C. *dág-pa*, W. *rág-će* to lay, to put, a thing over or by the side of an other, ṕyogs-gcíg-tu (phyogs gcig tu) Lex.; gen. used only in the gerund: *t́sa dor rág-ne* together with salt and spices W.; *ńá-źa dań rág-te mi dug* he does not belong to us W., or in compounds: nyi-rág (nyi rag) double-barreled gun (one barrel beside the other), W. *rań-bár d́ug-rág* six-barreled pistol, revolver W., bse-sgo bdun-sbrag (bse sgo bdun sbrag) Pth., sevenfold skin-door, used as a target for shooting at.

C. *d́ań-bu*, W. *ráń-ńu, ra-un* fly, and similar insects without a sting; sbráń-ma (sbrang ma) 1. id. 2. C. bee, sbráń-mai tsogs (sbrang mai tshogs) swarm of bees. — sbráń-rtsi (sbrang rtsi) W. *ráń-si* honey; *ráń-si ráń-ńu* W. bee. — sbráń-čań (sbrang chang) mead or something similar. — sbráń-tsán (sbrang tshang) and sbráń-doń (sbrang dong) Cs. cells in a honey-comb, the honey-comb itself. — sbráń-bug (sbrang bug) bee-hive Sch. — sbráń-byí (sbrang byi) marten Sch. — sbráń-yáb (sbrang yab) flap, fly-brush Cs.

1. to sneeze Med.; sbríd-pa byuń (sbrid pa byung) I am seized with a sneezing Med. — 2. to become numb, torpid, *káń-pe ńya d́id soń* my foot is asleep W. — 3. Dzl. 2S, 5 Sch. to flutter before one's eyes (?).

pf. and imp. sbrus (sbrus), fut. and sec. form sbru (sbru), W *rú-će* to stir with one's hand, zan (zan) Lex.; to knead (Cs.) is rdzí-ba (rdzi ba) which is not identical with sbrúd-pa (sbrud pa), at least not in W.

pregnant, big with young; mi dań srog-čags sbrum-ma-rnams (mi dang srog chags sbrum ma rnams) Dzl. women with child and beasts with young; sbrúm-par gyúr-ba (sbrum par gyur ba) to conceive, to become pregnant, frq.; sbrúm-par tsór-na (sbrum par tshor na) feeling pregnant Pth.; ṕrú-gu sbrum byuń-bas (phru gu sbrum byung bas) having conceived, being with child Pth.

Pur. sbrul (sbrul), Lh. *rúl*, C. *d́ul* 1. serpent, snake; sbrul (sbrul) and sbrúl-mo (sbrul mo) also mythical demoniac beings; sbrul gdúg-pa (sbrul gdug pa) or dug-sbrul (dug sbrul) venomous serpent; sbrul k'as sdíg-po (sbrul khas sdig po) Sch. serpent-tamer; sbrúl-gyi snyiń-po (sbrul gyi snying po) v. tsán-dan (tsan dan). — sbrúl-mgo (sbrul mgo) 1. a serpent's head. 2. v. ań-ke (ang ke). — sbrul-sgóń (sbrul sgong) a serpent's egg. — *d́ul-nyá* eel or some other eculent snake-like fish C. — sbrul-dug (sbrul dug) venom of serpents. — sbrul-mig (sbrul mig) 1. a snake's eye. 2. n. of a certain vein Med. — sbrul-tsil (sbrul tshil) snake's grease Med. — sbrul-źágs (sbrul zhags) v. źágs (zhags). — sbrul-ló (sbrul lo) serpent-year, sbrúl-lo-pa (sbrul lo pa) one born in such a year v. lo (lo). — sbrul-sun (sbrul shun) slough, skin of a snake. — 2. symb. num.: 8, = klu (klu).
Lex. n. of an animal; Sch.: stone-fox.

wylie: sbre bo, re bo, re ba
english: a coarse material manufactured of yak's hair for tent-coverings.
pf. sbreńs (sbrengs), Cs.: to play an instrument; acc. to Dzl. SV, 16, to jerk a chord, a bow-string.
Cs.: resp. for ltógs-pa (ltogs pa) hungry.

W. *rél-ce(s)* to stitch together, paper; to stitch to, to sew on; to fasten on, a package on a horse; lcágs-sgog lág-pa sbrél-nas (lcags sgog lag pa sbrel nas) having one's hands shackled together; bar zám-gyis sbrel (bar zam gyis sbrel) the chasm is overarched by a bridge Glr.; (iron chains) séń-ge dań (seng ge dang) fastened to (stone) lions; in a gen. sense: to connect, to join, nyís-sbrél, gsum-sbrél (nyis sbrel, gsum sbrel) two or three consonants joined together, cf. miń-gźi (ming gzhi).

pf. and fut. sbran (sbran) 1. to call to the spot, rá-mda, grogs (ra mda, grogs) for assistance Lex.; to send for, the minister Glr. — 2. to call to Thgy.; to give information, notice, intelligence, rgyál-po-la rmí-lam-du (rgyal po la rmi lam du) to warn the king by a dream Dzl.; mi źig sbrán-te du btáń-nas (mi zhig sbran te du btang nas) Dzl. to dispatch a man in order to convey intelligence. — 3. to sprinkle, to stain, to pollute, tíg-les (tig les) Sch.
